title: Understanding and Improving Data Repurposing

dataset_schema.label: Neighbourhood Nestwatch (citizen-generated reports of birds in backyards (citizen-generated reports of birds in backyards))
dataset_schema.description: location data, observation metadata (species, count, date, location, observer), species data, breeding/nest data, effort metrics (duration, distance, area).

dataset_schema.label: World atlas of artificial night sky brightness
dataset_schema.description: Ratio to natural brightness, Artificial brightness (μcd/m²), Approximate total brightness (mcd/m²) of 1777m resolution, Anthropogenic noise data of expected sound pressures of 270m resolution

original_intent.label: World atlas
original_intent.description: systematically quantify and map global light pollution levels to support scientific research and understand the environmental and societal impacts of artificial night sky brightness.

repurposed_use: Repurposed during COVID-19 lockdowns to study the effect of reduced human activity and light pollution on songbird physiological health by augmenting with geospatial light/noise datasets.

limitations.label: Intra-data Inconsistenty
limitations.description: Data collected with inconsistent effort across participants.

limitations.label: Granularity
limitations.description: Data could only match the coarsest resolution, leading to loss of potentially valuable information.

transformations.label: Join
transformations.description: Geospatial light pollution maps, noise pollution layers, land cover datasets

notes: Introduces the concept of data 'elasticity': the degree to which data can be stretched to fit new schemas. Distinguishes repurposing (schema change required) from reuse (existing schema sufficient). Identifies actors, enablers, and constraints shaping the repurposing ecosystem.
